question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the metric field contains a cluster value with Unicode characters requiring expensive normalization in StringUtils.trimToNull(), causing CPU overhead per series entry that accumulates to above 30% resource increase across many series?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response size limits in prometheusClient.query(query) to cause the monitor node to receive and buffer an arbitrarily large HTTP response body from Prometheus, consuming heap memory and network bandwidth above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Prometheus response where resp.getSeries() returns a list with a very large number of entries all having null metric fields, causing the filter(Objects::nonNull) and toClusterLagEntry() null checks to execute on every entry, consuming CPU in tight loop iterations above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request deduplication in health() so that N simultaneous callers each independently trigger a full Prometheus query and full parseLagByCluster() stream pipeline, causing N-fold CPU and memory amplification that exceeds 30% resource increase?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: parseDouble()] Can an unprivileged external user inject Prometheus series where raw values are Java special float strings like 'NaN', 'Infinity', or '-Infinity', causing parseDouble() to return successfully but isLagAboveThreshold() to call Double.isFinite() on every value, and if combined with large series counts, drive CPU usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() is invoked by Spring Boot's health aggregation on every /actuator/health request, and by triggering many concurrent health requests, cause the monitor node's HTTP client connection pool to be exhausted, leading to connection creation overhead and elevated resource usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response with a series list containing tens of thousands of entries, causing the stream().filter().map().flatMap().collect() pipeline in parseLagByCluster() to allocate a large number of Optional and Map.Entry objects, triggering frequent minor GC cycles and increasing JVM CPU usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY uses a 3-minute rate window ([3m]) and is re-executed on every health check, causing Prometheus to scan large time-series windows repeatedly, resulting in large response payloads that consume monitor node network bandwidth and deserialization CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Prometheus response where the data.result() list contains deeply nested or very large PrometheusMetric objects, causing JSON deserialization in RestClient to allocate excessive heap memory per series entry, raising GC pressure and CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronous blocking I/O in SimpleClientHttpRequestFactory (used by PrometheusApiClient) to cause health() to block monitor threads for up to the full timeout duration, and by triggering many concurrent health checks, exhaust the thread pool and increase CPU scheduling overhead above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user, under preconditions where the cluster list is very large, cause buildLagQuery() to produce a PromQL query string with a very long regex alternation in the cluster=~\

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where each PrometheusSeries has a value list containing large Object instances (e.g., nested JSON objects deserialized as LinkedHashMap), causing String.valueOf(raw) in parseDouble() to invoke toString() on complex objects, consuming CPU and memory above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a result size cap in evaluate() to cause the monitor node to process an unbounded number of PrometheusSeries entries returned by Prometheus, with each entry requiring stream operations, Optional allocations, and HashMap insertions, driving heap and CPU usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() is called by Spring Boot Actuator's composite health check, which may aggregate multiple indicators, so that triggering /actuator/health causes ImporterLagHealthIndicator.health() to be invoked alongside other indicators, and the combined Prometheus query load raises total node resource usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Prometheus response where the series list contains entries with extremely long cluster label strings, causing lagByCluster.get(localCluster) and lagByCluster.remove(localCluster) to perform expensive string equality comparisons, and with many such entries, drive CPU usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response streaming in prometheusClient.query(query) (which buffers the full response body before deserialization) to cause the monitor node to allocate a large contiguous heap buffer for a massive Prometheus response, triggering full GC and raising resource usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where all series have null value fields, causing toClusterLagEntry() to check value == null for every entry and return Optional.empty(), and with a very large series list, the repeated null checks and Optional allocations drive CPU and memory above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() has no memoization or TTL-based caching, so that a monitoring framework or load balancer performing frequent liveness/readiness checks triggers a new Prometheus query on every poll interval, causing sustained elevated network and CPU resource usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where each PrometheusSeries has a value list with exactly 1 element (size < 2), causing toClusterLagEntry() to return Optional.empty() for every entry after the size check, and with a very large series list, the repeated list size checks and Optional allocations consume CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY embeds a cluster regex that grows with the number of configured clusters, and by triggering health() frequently, cause the monitor node to repeatedly serialize and transmit a large query string over HTTP, consuming network bandwidth and CPU above 30%?
